methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found official askUWA assessment guidance, editorial-assistance guidance, and an assessment responsibilities FAQ.
Firecrawl search found askUWA assessment and citation guidance, UWA Library AI guides, the 2026 student AI tools guide PDF, and a news article rejected as non-policy.

methodsAttempted.query: site:uwa.edu.au "Academic Integrity Policy" "Artificial Intelligence" "University of Western Australia"
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the official Academic Integrity Policy document and several lower-specificity course or news results.

Firecrawl search found UWA Library privacy guidance and the GenAI Think Tank page; the Think Tank page was rejected for policy-claim purposes because it presents strategic advice and recommendations rather than a binding rule.
Related askUWA links led to citation, editorial-assistance, and DeepSeek guidance pages.
UWA Library AI guide navigation led to ethical-considerations, acknowledge/reference, AI and research, and research-writing PDF guidance; one SharePoint webinar recording was marked login-required.

artifacts.summary: The crawl found official UWA public sources for student assessment AI permissions, AI-use tiers, academic integrity, AI citation and acknowledgement, AI editorial assistance, privacy/IP cautions, DeepSeek restrictions, and research-writing guidance. News, job, repository, strategy/recommendation, and login-required material was rejected or marked inaccessible.

stopConditions: Stop after official public UWA sources cover assessment AI permissions, academic integrity, citation/acknowledgement, privacy/IP, DeepSeek/security, and research-writing guidance.
Do not access login-required SharePoint, staff intranet, MFA, WAF, CAPTCHA, or non-public systems.
Do not use news, job postings, research repository records, or strategic recommendations as policy evidence.

artifacts.claimText: For UWA coursework assessments, AI tools may only be used where the Unit Coordinator explicitly permits them; permitted use must be cited, referenced, and clearly acknowledged.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow assessment-permission claim tied directly to askUWA Answer ID 3432.
Approved as descriptive student assessment guidance, not a canonical policy publication.
Approved because the Tier 1 wording and misconduct consequence are stated directly in the 2026 student guide.
Approved as a direct Academic Integrity Policy extraction. The claim does not add broader misconduct conclusions beyond the cited plagiarism clause.
Approved as askUWA guidance on limited editorial assistance. The wording avoids expanding it into blanket assessment permission.
Approved as a direct citation/reference guidance claim from askUWA Answer ID 3434.
Approved as a narrow student guide privacy/IP caution supported by concrete PDF text.
Approved as a specific tool/security claim about DeepSeek from official askUWA guidance.
Approved as research-writing guidance; the claim is not broadened into all research conduct beyond authorship and transparency.
Approved as research-writing input-compliance guidance tied directly to the UWA Library PDF.
Approved as UWA Library ethical guidance on university intellectual property; the claim remains a guidance candidate, not canonical policy.

artifacts.title: The University of Western Australia
artifacts.summary: The staged crawl found official UWA public evidence for assessment-specific AI permission, three AI-use tiers for assessment, Tier 1 no-AI academic misconduct treatment, Academic Integrity Policy treatment of AI-generated assessment material, AI citation and acknowledgement expectations, limited editorial-assistance guidance, privacy/IP cautions for public AI systems, DeepSeek restrictions, and UWA Library research-writing guidance on AI authorship and compliant inputs.
limitations: This report is based on public official sources retrieved on 2026-05-13 UTC for run uapt-the-university-of-western-australia-20260513.
This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The GenAI Think Tank page was rejected for claim extraction because it presents strategic advice and recommendations rather than binding policy.
A SharePoint-linked research webinar recording was not accessed because it required UWA login.
The staged claims are agent-reviewed candidates only and are not marked human-reviewed or canonical.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
